Ad Text:
The Opportunity 

QIC is seeking a senior leader to take ownership of our newly created position -  Head of Enterprise Initiative & Governance. This is a high-impact role at the centre of our organisation, responsible for ensuring QIC is focused on the right priorities and delivering on its strategy. 

This is a rare opportunity to build and shape a new enterprise function from the ground up within a leading global investment organisation. 

Reporting to the Chief Financial and Operating Officer (CFOO), this role will bring greater clarity, structure and discipline to how work is prioritised, governed and delivered across the business. It will provide a unique opportunity to shape how QIC executes its strategy, with direct visibility to senior leadership and enterprise-wide influence. 

About you 

You will play a critical role in connecting strategy to execution, bringing together enterprise prioritisation, portfolio oversight and performance insight to enable better decision-making and stronger outcomes. In this role, you will also help align investment, client, corporate and government priorities, ensuring a clear and consistent strategic narrative for key stakeholders. 

Using your previous experience of establishing an enterprise-wide PMO, you will create capability, and visibility across all initiatives, ensuring they are prioritised, sequenced and resourced effectively. Working closely with senior leaders across QIC, you will drive a more coordinated and disciplined approach to delivery, improving how the organisation makes decisions and allocates resources. 

The role offers significant scope to influence how QIC operates at an enterprise level, balancing strategic thinking with hands-on execution, and working across a complex, high-performing environment. 

Key Responsibilities 

Translate QIC’s strategy into clear, actionable enterprise priorities, ensuring alignment across investment, corporate and government initiatives 

Develop and embed an enterprise-wide prioritisation framework, balancing strategic alignment, value, risk, resourcing and sequencing of work 

Establish and lead an enterprise PMO capability, creating visibility across all initiatives and improving governance, consistency and delivery outcomes 

Provide a consolidated, enterprise-wide view of strategic initiatives, enabling informed decision-making at ExCo and Board level 

Implement structured governance including business case standards, stage gates, approval pathways and measures of success 

Drive benefits realisation, post-implementation reviews and continuous improvement across initiatives 

Partner with Finance and People & Culture to align strategy execution with budgets, workforce planning and capacity 

Define and track enterprise KPIs and performance metrics, linking strategy execution to outcomes and accountability 

Provide independent challenge and insight to ensure initiatives deliver value and align with strategic priorities 

Act as a central connector across the organisation, working closely with senior stakeholders to drive alignment, prioritisation and execution discipline 

Key Criteria

Tertiary qualifications in Business, Finance, or related discipline 

10+ years’ experience in strategy execution, portfolio management or business performance  

Strong commercial and project management skills 

Highly developed analytical and problem-solving skills including a solution orientated mindset  

Demonstrated experience operating in complex, multi-stakeholder environments  

Excellent prioritisation and organisational skills 

Strong written and interpersonal skills with demonstrated ability to influence and negotiate at senior leadership levels 




If you're interested in this opportunity, please submit your cover letter and resume promptly as we will be reviewing candidates as they apply. 




QIC is sourcing directly for this role and is not engaging recruitment agencies. We do not accept unsolicited resumes, and QIC is not responsible for any fees associated with unsolicited submissions.




About QIC:

As one of Australia’s leading institutional investment managers with more than A$100billion in assets under management, we have a presence across four continents, seven offices and assets across global markets.   




At QIC, we believe in shared value — where delivering strong investment returns can go hand in hand with doing good for our clients, people and communities. If we invest well, we will all do better together. 

Life at QIC:

We know that our people perform at their best when they feel a sense of belonging and are encouraged to bring their authentic selves to work every day.  We provide a supportive work environment where everyone can thrive and feel valued for their meaningful contribution in delivering for the prosperity of our clients, people and communities.

Joining QIC, you can contribute to our employee led networks that champion our diversity, equity and inclusion strategy, which consists of five key pillars: Gender Equality, LGBTQ+, Accessibility, Cultural & Linguistic Diversity, and First Nations People.

Other reasons to join us:

Flexibility and hybrid options encouraged – certified Family Inclusive Workplace

Recharge leave – additional 3 days leave per year

Annual fitness/home office reimbursement

Ability to swap public holidays for those of personal, cultural or religious significance

26 weeks gender-neutral paid parental leave

Financial study assistance and study leave

Community leave for supporting the causes you love

Development opportunities to extend, expand and enhance your growth

At QIC, we welcome and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, the LGBTQI+ community, people with disabilities and people from all cultural backgrounds. We have an equitable, barrier-free application process so please inform us of your accessibility requirements in your application or you can reach out to our Talent Team on 07 3360 3800

Job Title: BI Team lead - Business Performance Analytics
Ad Text:
BI Team Lead | Data & Analytics Leadership

Are you a technically strong BI leader ready to take full ownership of an enterprise analytics function? This is a genuine leadership role, where your work directly shapes how decisions get made at the highest level. You'll be joining a collaborative team with a modern cloud stack, a clear roadmap, and real investment in doing things properly.

About the Role

As BI Team Lead - Business Performance Analytics, you'll lead a talented team responsible for delivering high quality data products and analytical models that support smarter decisions across the business. Day to day you'll be working closely with Data Engineering and Platform teams, turning business requirements into structured project plans, and holding the bar high on technical output. You'll also drive Agile delivery, facilitating SCRUM processes and keeping your team aligned to both BAU and strategic roadmap priorities.

About You

You bring at least 7 years of IT experience, including 2+ years leading technical teams, and a solid background in BI, reporting, data modelling and analytics. You know how to get into the detail of a complex data model and can just as confidently walk an executive through the findings in plain language. Your experience includes:

Leading and developing high-performing technical teams
Analytical data modelling and manipulation at scale
Microsoft Power BI and the broader Azure ecosystem: Azure Data Factory, Databricks, SQL Server and DevOps
Programming in Python, R or PySpark for data processing and automation

A relevant tertiary qualification is expected.

Thank you for reading! Please apply directly, or for a quicker response, contact Ethan Brown (Talenza) through LinkedIn.

We're looking for an experienced Senior Business Analyst with deep expertise in HR systems, across Core HR, Recruitment, Talent Management, and Learning. If you're passionate about shaping impactful products, this is your chance to help build the next generation of HR technology.

You'll drive innovative solutions and help evolve HR products designed for an AI-powered world, identifying opportunities where automation, predictive insights, and generative AI can simplify processes and enhance decision-making.

As a Senior BA at TechnologyOne, you'll work closely with customers, internal teams, and domain experts to turn complex, people-centric requirements into practical, customer-focused outcomes. You'll contribute across the full product lifecycle - from discovery and design through testing, rollout, and adoption - ensuring our HRP products are intuitive, compliant, and trusted by the organisations that rely on them.

What you'll be doing…
Applying deep HR domain expertise across Core HR, Recruitment, Onboarding, Talent, Learning, Performance, and compliance, with insight into trends shaping the Education and Local Government sectors.
Gathering customer and market insights by partnering closely with Consulting, Sales, Support, and customers to inform product direction.
Working with Product Design and Development to refine requirements, design new features, and deliver high-quality enhancements.
Conducting Business Acceptance Testing to ensure releases are compliant, stable, intuitive, and aligned to real-world HR processes.
Creating clear enablement content - documentation, learning resources, and release materials - to support internal and customer adoption.
Participating in presentations, forums, and showcases to highlight new features and share insights with stakeholders.

Your Talents…
Relevant degree and/or extensive experience as a Business Analyst, ideally within HR systems, talent platforms, ATS, or LMS solutions.
Strong hands-on understanding of HR modules - especially Recruitment, Talent Management, Learning & Development, and wider employee lifecycle processes.
Knowledge of HR compliance, modern people practices, and process optimisation (TechnologyOne HRP experience highly regarded).
Strong capability in analysis tools, techniques, and methodologies, with the ability to translate complexity into clarity.
Exceptional communication, facilitation, and stakeholder engagement skills.
Excellent time-management and prioritisation skills in a fast-paced product environment.

Benefits…
Attractive remuneration package with competitive benefits.
Industry-leading employee share plan, giving you the opportunity to invest in our success.
Award-winning culture - 8x Australian Business Awards "Employer of Choice" winner.
Inclusive and vibrant workplace - we celebrate diversity and love our team events.
Flexible working hours - we value work-life balance and support family-friendly arrangements.
Free gym membership in your region, or if you're at HQ, enjoy our state-of-the-art, 24/7 exclusive onsite gym.
Additional 2.5 days of leave annually to volunteer for a charity of your choice.
Onsite perks - enjoy free breakfast and barista-made coffee every day.
Structured career growth - clear pathways for progression with a defined career framework.

Why Join us?

At TechnologyOne, we're big on creating an inclusive, supportive environment where people can do their best work. We value different perspectives and back our people with real opportunities to grow, competitive salaries, and clear career pathways.

Our culture is built around creativity, innovation and giving people the space to make an impact. From team events to charity initiatives, it's a place people genuinely enjoy being part of.

Who we are…

TechnologyOne (ASX: TNE) is Australia's largest enterprise software company and one of Australia's top 100 ASX-listed companies, with locations across six countries. We provide a global SaaS ERP solution that transforms business and makes life simple for our customers. Our deeply integrated enterprise SaaS solution is available on any device, anywhere and anytime and is incredibly easy to use. Over 1,200 leading corporations, government agencies, local councils and universities are powered by our software.

For more than 36 years, we have been providing our customers with enterprise software that evolves and adapts to new and emerging technologies, allowing them to focus on their business and not technology.

xAmplify – Leading Australia’s Digital Evolution

Workday Functional Consultant

At xAmplify, we’re not just another technology company.

We are shaping Australia’s future by combining intelligent automation and human‑centred technology services to deliver exceptional value.

Our vision is bold, to lead Australia’s digital evolution by accelerating the safe and secure adoption of Automation and AI. We partner with the world’s most innovative technology companies to transform businesses, industries, and government services. Every project is an opportunity to transform operations, solve real‑world problems, and push the boundaries of what’s possible.

We are enablers. What’s ours is yours, check us out at xAmplify.com.au

About the Role

xAmplify is building its Workday capability and this role is the first hire on our Workday team.

We’re looking for a senior, hands‑on Workday Functional Consultant who wants more than just another implementation role. This is a unique opportunity to design, own, and scale our internal Workday environment across Finance and HR, then play a key role as we gradually expand into external consulting services.

If you enjoy autonomy, influence, and building something meaningful from scratch, this role was created for you.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

As our founding Workday consultant, you will:

Lead end‑to‑end Workday implementations and optimisation across Workday HCM and Workday Financials
Act as the internal subject matter expert for Workday, partnering closely with HR, Finance, IT, and senior stakeholders
Gather business requirements and translate them into scalable Workday configurations
Configure, test, deploy, and support Workday modules across HR and Finance
Own functional design decisions, system documentation, training materials, and governance
Support data validation, testing cycles, and user adoption activities
Advise leaders on best practices, system enhancements, and roadmap planning
Help establish the foundations for a future Workday consulting practice, including mentoring and capability building as the team grows
Over time, support external client implementations and advisory work

What We’re Looking For

Essential Experience

7+ years of Workday implementation experience
Proven experience implementing both Workday HCM and Workday Financials
Active Workday certifications in HCM and Financials
Experience leading full lifecycle implementations (requirements through post go‑live support)
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Ability to operate autonomously and make senior‑level design decisions

Highly Regarded

Experience building or leading a Workday function or team
Consulting or client‑facing Workday experience
Knowledge of integrations, security, reporting, or analytics
A builder mindset — comfortable with ambiguity and growth

Why join us?

Ready to play a key role in Australia’s digital evolution? Apply today or reach out to us at recruitment@xamplify.com.au, we’d love to hear from you. The future isn’t just coming—we’re creating it.

We offer flexible working options where possible, with strong support for wellbeing and family balance. Employer‑funded perks, benefits, rewards, and recognition through Employment Hero. Strong career pathways and professional development opportunities — including certifications and growth into areas like automation, cyber, cloud or advanced support. A connected national team with regular engagement, support, and learning opportunities. A culture grounded in diversity, inclusion, and community impact through the xAmplify Foundation.

Equal Opportunity Statement

Diversity fuels innovation. At xAmplify, we welcome applications from all backgrounds, identities, and abilities. xAmplify is an advocate for diversity and inclusion, and welcomes applications from all irrespective of their gender, LGBTQI identity, ethnicity, religion, physical ability or age.

Title: S/4HANA Finance and Controlling (FICO) Functional Expert

Contract duration: Until 19 March 2027

Location: Richlands or Brisbane CBD

Join a specialist delivery team within the Queensland Government, focused on a high-profile transformation project to commission a state-of-the-art facility. This role is pivotal in designing and implementing fit-for-purpose S/4HANA FICO solutions that underpin critical operational supply chain processes. You will collaborate with architects and stakeholders to ensure a seamless transition to a modern enterprise software system, ensuring operational efficiency and long-term scalability.

What You Will Do:

Design and implement new S/4HANA FICO configurations and WRICEF objects in alignment with business requirements and SAP best practices.

Develop S/4HANA master data structures, including general ledger accounts, profit centres, and cost centres, ensuring alignment with overall solution architecture.

Lead the integration of FICO modules with related systems such as Materials Management (MM), Sales and Distribution (SD), and Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).

Deep technical understanding of FICO integration with VIM, MM, SD, EWM, and Business Technology Platform (BTP).

Strong communication skills with the ability to translate complex technical requirements for both technical and non-technical stakeholders.




Interested? Apply Now!

About Us


We’re all here to deliver great retirement outcomes for our members. We have a long history with the University sector and today we’re a place where future‑minded Australians can all come together. We have a track record of success and a culture focussed on being the best place to work in superannuation. We’ll achieve this by bringing our values to life in everything we do – genuine care, being better together and a passion for outcomes. We have a clear plan of what we need to do and want you to help lead that journey.


Over 70% of all funds under management (FUM) are managed in‑house by our dedicated Investments team. We strongly believe our in‑house management strategy ultimately leads to greater retirement outcomes for our members through lower fees, sustainable investment options and top quartile investment performance.
This is an opportunity to build deep investment operations expertise in a high‑performing in‑house investment environment.
 

The role


The key focus of this role is to support effective portfolio management, trading, compliance and risk management through strong investment implementation, systems and control activities. Working within the Portfolio Implementation team, you will contribute to investment performance analysis, reporting and governance, while adopting an AI‑first approach to uplift efficiency and insight. Furthermore, you will: 

Support critical investment operations, systems and control functions across the Investments team
Act as a subject matter expert for core investment systems, particularly Bloomberg buy‑side solutions
Ensure accuracy, completeness and timeliness of portfolio, trade, compliance and collateral data
Support pre‑ and post‑trade compliance, trade matching and settlement processes
Contribute to investment performance, risk analysis and reporting
Support a strong internal control environment and audit readiness

About you


You bring strong technical capability across investment systems, data, controls and performance, with a proactive and detail‑focused approach. You enjoy working collaboratively with front office teams and service providers to resolve issues and continuously improve outcomes.

In addition, you will have:

Relevant tertiary qualifications in finance, economics, commerce or a related discipline
Experience in superannuation or funds management environments
Hands‑on experience with Bloomberg AIM, EMSX, CMGR or equivalent systems
Strong understanding of investment markets, trade lifecycle and compliance processes
Experience with custodians, collateral management and performance analysis
Strong problem‑solving, communication and stakeholder engagement skills

What We Offer


UniSuper believes that the best way to achieve great things is when we come together and collaborate. Therefore, we ask you to be able to commit to 60% of your time in office. UniSuper is proud of our culture and benefits, which empower our people to achieve their full potential, thrive, and grow their career with us. These include:


• Strong learning and development opportunities
• Competitive total rewards and superannuation
• Health, wellbeing and lifestyle benefits
 

It should go without saying, but at UniSuper, we value and celebrate diversity and inclusion. We believe that a variety of perspectives, backgrounds, interests, abilities, and skills is crucial for delivering great retirement outcomes for our members. We invite you to apply for the roles that suit your career aspirations, even if you don’t meet all the requirements.

Company Description

Nine is Australia’s largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you’ll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country’s most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands. 

Our content reaches almost every Australian - meaning what we do has real impact. We bring people together, celebrate the big moments, and capture the everyday ones.

Some of our most beloved brands have been part of Australian life for generations, and others - new on the scene - have already found their place firmly in our lives. We’re evolving and we need people like you to bring new ideas, innovate and make your mark.

Job Description

At Nine, we are harnessing the power of Artificial Intelligence to revolutionise our audience engagement and commercial strategies. As Head of AI Product, you will be at the forefront of this transformation, driving the development and implementation of innovative AI-powered products across our diverse portfolio.

This role is pivotal in shaping Nine’s AI strategy, translating complex AI capabilities into tangible product features that deliver exceptional value to our audiences, advertisers, and internal teams. You will be responsible for defining the product vision, building the roadmap, and leading a cross-functional team to deliver cutting-edge AI solutions.

Reporting to the Director, AI and Data Product, you will work closely with data scientists, engineers, and business stakeholders to identify and capitalise on opportunities to leverage AI in our media landscape.

As Head of AI Product, you will:

Define and champion the AI product vision and strategy: Align AI initiatives with Nine’s overall business objectives, identifying key opportunities to leverage AI for innovation and competitive advantage.

Lead the development of AI-powered products: Drive the end-to-end product lifecycle, from ideation and prototyping to launch and iteration, ensuring products are aligned with user needs and deliver measurable value.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams: Work closely with data scientists, engineers, designers, and business stakeholders to define requirements, prioritise features, and deliver high-quality AI solutions.

Build and maintain the AI product roadmap: Prioritise initiatives based on business impact, technical feasibility, and customer feedback, ensuring a clear and achievable roadmap.

Drive AI adoption across the organization: Evangelise the potential of AI, providing guidance and resources to teams across Nine to explore and integrate AI capabilities into their workflows.

Implement AI governance and ethical practices: Ensure AI products are developed and deployed responsibly, adhering to ethical guidelines and data privacy regulations

Analyse and measure product performance: Define and track key metrics to evaluate the success of AI products, using data-driven insights to inform product decisions and drive continuous improvement.

Stay ahead of the curve: Continuously research and evaluate emerging AI technologies and trends, identifying opportunities to incorporate them into Nine’s product strategy.

Qualifications

We are seeking a passionate and driven product leader with a strong understanding of AI and its potential to transform the media industry. You will have a proven track record of delivering successful AI products and a deep understanding of the product development lifecycle.

What you’ll bring to the role:

Experience:

5+ years of experience in product management, with a focus on AI or machine learning products.
Experience working in a media, technology, or data-driven organisation.
Experience leading cross-functional teams and managing complex projects.
Demonstrated experience bringing AI products from conception to launch.

Skills:

Deep understanding of AI and machine learning concepts and technologies.
Strong product vision and strategic thinking, with the ability to translate business objectives into actionable product roadmaps.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate with diverse teams.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to decision-making.
Understanding of data governance, privacy, and ethical considerations related to AI.Familiarity with agile product development methodologies.

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Masters or PhD in AI/ML is highly desirable.
Certifications in AI product management or machine learning are advantageous.
Additional Information

How we work

At Nine, our flexible work options vary by role and team. Depending on the position, this may include flexible hours, hybrid work, or part-time arrangements. We welcome discussing your flexibility needs during the hiring process - just ask the Talent Acquisition team. 

Our employee benefits include:

18 weeks paid parental leave with no distinction between primary and secondary carers.
Access to 'Employee Exclusives' program - a way of getting closer to our incredible brands, offering unique experiences, behind-the-scenes access, and awesome perks.
Digital newspaper subscription to our mastheads.
Annual gift voucher for Stan subscription.

More info at Nine Careers.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:

We're committed to a safe, respectful and inclusive Nine. From day one, you'll be encouraged to bring your whole self to work and will be supported to perform at your best.

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities, and of all ages, genders, nationalities, backgrounds and cultures as we recognise the importance and value of diverse perspectives. Should you require any adjustments to the recruitment process, please advise us when you apply.

Work rights: Please note to apply for this role you must already have the right to lawfully work and live in Australia.

THE ORGANISATION 
Our client is a leading financial services institution with a strong reputation for stability, innovation, and a forward-thinking approach. As part of ongoing investment in strategic workforce capabilities, they are seeking a data-driven Senior HR Reporting Specialist to join their People Analytics team. This is an excellent opportunity to contribute to an enterprise-wide agenda focused on workforce insights, governance, and risk-aware decision-making.

PLEASE NOTE THIS IS A 3-6 MONTH CONTRACT. 

THE ROLE 
Sitting within the Remuneration function the Senior HR Reporting Specialist will take the lead on designing and delivering accurate, timely, and impactful people reporting across the business. You’ll support regulatory and internal reporting requirements, workforce planning, and strategic talent initiatives with high-quality data and analysis. You will manage cyclical and ad-hoc HR reporting, including headcount, attrition, span of control, diversity, compliance training, and workforce cost analysis as well as developing and enhancing dashboards and visual reporting using Power BI, ensuring data is clean and consistent. 

THE CRITERIA 
You are a highly analytical HR data professional with a strong understanding of reporting within a regulated and metrics-driven environment. You thrive in complex organisational structures and are comfortable influencing at senior levels with data-led storytelling. Proven experience in HR reporting, people analytics or workforce planning within financial services or a similarly regulated industry. Proficiency in Excel, Power BI, and ideally experience with HRIS platforms such as Workday is essential. 
You must be immediately available to be considered for this role. 

Please contact Emma Dundas by emailing emma@peoplecorp.com.au for more information or please apply below or through our website www.peoplecorp.com.au.

Join ING’s Mortgage Tribe and help shape the pricing, modelling, and strategic direction of one of Australia’s most competitive mortgage businesses. This role is perfect for a data‑driven leader who thrives on complexity, has deep modelling expertise, and wants to influence enterprise‑level pricing decisions.

What you’ll do

Lead the design, enhancement, and adoption of advanced pricing and elasticity models that directly influence ING’s mortgage strategy.

Build accurate forecasting frameworks and dashboards that provide clear insights across NIM, ROE, growth, attrition, and portfolio performance.

Provide strategic pricing recommendations through high‑quality analysis, market intelligence, and competitor insights.

Shape and uplift ING’s pricing‑engine capability by defining the target state, core foundations, and high‑value use cases.

Partner with Finance, Treasury, Product, Distribution, CX and senior stakeholders to ensure pricing actions balance commercial outcomes and customer value.

What we’re looking for

7+ years’ experience in pricing, analytics, modelling, or portfolio optimisation — ideally in financial services.

Strong technical capability across statistical modelling tools (Python, SQL, SAS, R) and data visualisation platforms (Power BI, Tableau).

Deep understanding of mortgage products, pricing levers, and profitability drivers (NIM, ROE, margin, mix).

Proven ability to communicate complex analytical insights clearly to senior stakeholders and governance forums.

Someone who thrives in a fast‑paced environment, shows exceptional attention to detail, and can balance strategic thinking with hands‑on execution.

Discounted ING Health Insurance.

An additional Rest Day to support your wellbeing.

An IMPACT Day to volunteer on approved sustainability activity.

The ideal candidate will possess strong analytical & process mapping skills, proficiency in requirements management tools like JIRA and Confluence, and the ability to manage multiple priorities under pressure. About the Role

We are seeking an experienced AI Business Analyst to support the delivery of AI-enabled products and services within a Federal Government environment.

This role sits within a multi-disciplinary Agile team, where you’ll bridge business needs and AI/data capabilities—translating complex problems into clear, testable requirements while ensuring solutions are ethical, transparent and compliant.

Key Responsibilities

You will:

Elicit, analyse and document business, data and AI requirements

Translate requirements into epics, features and user stories with clear acceptance criteria

Define AI-specific non-functional requirements (e.g. transparency, privacy, robustness, security)

Map business processes and decision points to determine AI vs rules-based approaches

Support design of AI evaluation frameworks (performance, drift, fairness)

Collaborate with data scientists, engineers and product owners to shape AI solutions

Lead workshops, discovery sessions and backlog refinement activities

Develop test cases for AI/ML features, including A/B testing and validation approaches

Document AI guardrails, prompt design and safety constraints

Support governance activities including privacy, risk and compliance assessments

Contribute to change management, communications and user adoption initiatives

About You

You will bring:

3+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst or Systems Analyst in Agile environments

Hands-on experience delivering AI-enabled solutions (e.g. ML, NLP, LLMs, Copilot)

Strong ability to write testable user stories and acceptance criteria for AI features

Understanding of responsible AI principles (privacy, explainability, bias mitigation, human-in-the-loop)

Experience facilitating workshops and engaging diverse stakeholders

Proficiency with Azure DevOps, Miro (or similar tools)

Excellent communication skills and ability to influence decision-making

Why Apply?

Work on cutting-edge AI initiatives within Federal Government

Contribute to ethical and responsible AI delivery

Collaborative Agile environment with multidisciplinary teams

Flexible hybrid working arrangements

Apply Now

If you’re an experienced Business Analyst with a passion for AI and emerging technologies, apply now.

At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. Your New Role

We're looking for a Senior Analyst - Risk Enablement to join our Group Risk team on an 18-month fixed term contract, based in Melbourne with hybrid working.

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Risk Strategy & Enablement, this role offers variety and exposure across multiple risk domains. You'll provide second line support to subject matter experts and leaders, contributing to both business-as-usual activities and strategic initiatives. Key duties include but are not limited to:

Support Group Risk SMEs and Leads with BAU tasks across five risk departments.
Support implementation and embedment of risk transformation projects.
Assist with controls testing, risk assessments, and policy updates.
Perform data analysis and reporting to inform decision-making.
Maintain GRC records and coordinate workshops, training, and policy reviews.
Develop training materials and collateral for risk initiatives.
Manage the Group Risk Enablement workplan and associated reporting.
Coordinate monthly communications such as the 'In the Know' Bulletin and 3 Lines Forum.
Promote compliance and risk awareness across the organisation.


What You'll Need

3+ years' experience in risk management within financial services or consulting.
Strong understanding of risk and control practices, frameworks, and assurance activities.
Experience in risk assessments, control reviews, and risk reporting.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office, especially Excel and PowerPoint.
Critical thinking and analytical skills with a continuous improvement mindset.
Excellent communication skills and ability to build collaborative relationships.
Tertiary qualifications in Business, Commerce, Law or a related field.
Experience with RSA Archer GRC tool and/or PowerBI.
Exposure to risk in change programs.
Accreditation or willingness to obtain accreditation in Risk Management (e.g., RMIA or GIA).
Ability to translate regulatory requirements (e.g., APRA) into practical business outcomes.

Lead IRAP Specialist (Flexible Contract / T&M)
Location - Canberra, Melbourne, Sydney, Brisbane, Adelaide (Hybrid)
Job Title - Lead IRAP Specialist (Contract – Flexible Engagement)
Rates - $165 – $185 per hour (inc. super) – depending on experience and IRAP status

About Us
Canberra Consulting focuses on delivering highly skilled and qualified professionals into Federal Government environments. We understand secure delivery, procurement frameworks and what it takes to operate effectively in complex ICT and security environments.
We focus on fit — for both our clients and our candidates — ensuring you are engaged on meaningful work that aligns with your expertise.

The Opportunity
We are looking to bolster our current virtual bench of experienced IRAP professionals to support a pipeline of upcoming and ad hoc assessment engagements across Federal Government and Defence.
This is not a traditional full-time contract. Instead, you will be engaged on a Time and Materials basis, brought in to deliver targeted IRAP assessments, audits and advisory services as required.
You will operate as an independent specialist, supporting agencies to assess and uplift their security posture in alignment with the ISM and PSPF, and enabling risk-based authorisation decisions.
Engagements may vary in duration, complexity and location, with flexible working arrangements available.

Key Responsibilities

Deliver end-to-end IRAP assessments across Commonwealth ICT environments
Define assessment scope, system boundaries and data classifications (up to SECRET)
Evaluate security controls through documentation review, stakeholder interviews and technical validation
Identify risks, control gaps and non-compliance with ISM and PSPF
Produce IRAP Assessment Reports and Security Control Matrices
Provide independent, evidence-based advice to support system authorisation decisions
Maintain independence while advising on risk mitigation and security uplift strategies
Essential Criteria

Demonstrated experience delivering full IRAP assessments within Federal Government
Strong experience across complex ICT environments and security architectures
Deep understanding of the ISM, PSPF and Australian Government security frameworks
Current or recent IRAP Assessor status
Desirable Experience

Experience working in DISP-aligned or high-security environments
Ability to operate independently and engage senior stakeholders
Working Arrangements

Flexible, engagement-based model (not full-time)
Work delivered on a Time & Materials basis
Opportunities across ACT, VIC, NSW, QLD and SA
Hybrid delivery depending on engagement
Security Clearance
You must be an Australian Citizen and  hold NV1 clearance

How to Apply
If you are an experienced IRAP professional looking for flexible, high-impact engagements, we would like to hear from you.
Apply now or reach out to Alister at Canberra Consulting for a confidential discussion.

Do you enjoy solving complex data challenges, leading meaningful projects, and helping people turn ideas into impact? Are you comfortable connecting big-picture strategy to managing details, timelines, and teams? If so, you might be our next Engagement Manager.

At InterWorks, we help clients make sense of data, AI, and analytics. As an Engagement Manager, you'll combine your technical expertise with strong project leadership to guide clients through data transformation. You'll connect strategy to execution and ideas to outcomes, bringing clarity, collaboration, and craft to every engagement.  This role is equal parts architect, advisor, and project manager. You'll help shape solutions, oversee delivery, and ensure that every project we take on reflects the best of InterWorks.

Location Preferences: We’d love for you to join us from one of our offices in Melbourne or Sydney.  

Salary Range: $100,000 - $150,000 AUD depending on experience and qualifications.

What You’ll Do

Lead analytics and data projects from start to finish, ensuring outcomes align with client goals

Serve as the main point of contact for clients, managing expectations, communication, and long-term relationships

Translate strategy into delivery plans, scoping, scheduling, and coordinating across teams to bring solutions to life

Provide project management oversight to ensure timelines, budgets, and quality stay on track

Act as a subject matter expert within projects for your area of focus (BI, data engineering, cloud, or governance)

Conceptualize, design, and deliver scalable analytics solutions using modern data tools and platforms

Collaborate with leadership on proposals and pre-sales, shaping scopes of work and delivery approaches

Support change management and help clients adopt new technologies, processes, and mindsets

Mentor consultants and engineers, guiding both project work and professional growth

What You’ll Need

Five or more years of experience delivering analytics or data solutions in a consulting or enterprise environment

Proven ability to lead complex projects with multiple stakeholders and dependencies

Strong project management skills — PMP, Agile, or similar certification is a plus

Hands-on experience with analytics and database tools such as Tableau, Power BI, ThoughtSpot, Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks

Familiarity with data orchestration and transformation tools (dbt, Matillion, Fivetran, Alteryx, or Python)

Comfort with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud)

Excellent communication and storytelling skills, from executive briefings to technical discussions

Experience with pre-sales processes and scoping project work

A track record of bringing clarity to complexity and driving projects to completion

A collaborative mindset that builds strong partnerships across teams and with clients

What We’d Like You to Have

Experience leading data modernisation or governance initiatives

A background in analytics platform design or embedded analytics

Change management or organisational transformation experience

A passion for mentoring others and helping teams grow and deliver great work together

What We Offer

Health Benefits: Our people are our most important asset. That's why we offer an exceptional health insurance subsidy through BUPA, ensuring you and your family are well taken care of.

Wellness Benefits: We want you to stay healthy! Our employees receive an annual allowance to spend on their favourite gym memberships, sports activities, or workout apps.

Work-Life Balance: We offer a life balance allowance, encouraging our employees to relax and recharge — whether with family or going solo.

Tech Choice: Windows or Mac? 13-inch or 15-inch? Lightweight or heavy-duty? You know what works best for you. Our employees have a wide choice of equipment, plus a contribution towards standing desk equipment.

Professional Growth: We invest in our people. You'll have access to ongoing training, certifications, and professional development opportunities to keep your skills sharp and your career moving forward.

Community Impact: We InterWorkers like to use our powers for good. As well as undertaking pro bono consulting work, we support our colleagues' individual charitable passions with dedicated time off for volunteering.

Superannuation: Compulsory superannuation contributions are paid on top of your base salary in accordance with Australian legislation.




Why InterWorks

InterWorks is a people-focused tech consultancy that empowers clients with customized, collaborative solutions, and we love pursuing innovation alongside people who inspire us. Our approach to work and community is unique and unconventional—just like us—and that’s the way we want it. The only thing missing is you.  At InterWorks, we value unique contributions, our people are the glue that holds our business together.  We’re always looking for the right people, and we could be your perfect fit.

Senior Data Engineer | 12-Month Contract

Location: Sydney (Hybrid/On-site)

Rate: Excellent Daily Rate

Industry: Reputed Banking & Financial Services

The Opportunity

As a Data Engineer, you will be a key contributor to the design and development of high-performance data pipelines and platform features. This role sits at the heart of our transformational data initiatives, where you will apply advanced problem-solving skills to build practical, well-structured solutions that underpin the bank's future data capabilities.


Key Responsibilities

Feed Configuration: Design and write complex configurations for diverse data feeds, ensuring seamless integration and data consistency across the platform.

Pipeline Engineering: Build and maintain scalable, reliable data pipelines using Python, SQL, and dbt.

Cloud Architecture: Develop and optimize data solutions within AWS, specifically leveraging Amazon Redshift.

Stakeholder Alignment: Work directly with business and operational stakeholders to translate complex requirements into structured technical solutions.

What You Offer (Technical Stack)

Orchestration: Experience with pipeline tools such as Airflow or Argo Workflows.

Big Data & Integration: Familiarity with APIs and big data technologies (Spark, Hive, or Presto).

Security & CI/CD: A solid understanding of security best practices and version control using Bitbucket.

Preferred Experience

Banking Background: Previous experience in a banking or highly regulated financial environment is highly preferred.

Ad Text:
Opportunity to work with one of the largest General Insurer in Australia

Open for Sydney candidates only - Hybrid work flexibility

Lead and grow a high-performing analytics team to deliver impactful insights

About Hollard

Hollard Insurance is part of the Hollard Group of Companies and one of largest General Insurer in Australia and New Zealand. We underwrite a broad range of general insurance products, including motor, home, contents, business, bicycle and pet, in proud partnership with some of Australia's biggest consumer brands and broker businesses. As an independent and privately owned company, we believe we can go further together – for our people, customers, communities and partners – through an innovative approach to providing insurance. 

Our state-of-the-art facilities in North Sydney reflect our commitment to providing innovative workspaces that support hybrid and remote work.

Your team

You'll be joining the Chief Data Analytics Office, a specialised team of analytical experts who help drive data-driven decision making across Hollard. We're a collaborative group of technical specialists who thrive on transforming complex data into actionable business insights that deliver measurable impact. Our team is built on a foundation of analytical rigour, continuous innovation, and strategic thinking – focused on developing enterprise-wide intelligence capabilities that support business growth and operational excellence.

Your role

Reporting to the Business Insights & Reporting Lead, you will contribute to the success of the function through the following key responsibilities:e

Your duties and responsibilities

Execute enterprise business intelligence strategies in partnership with the Business Insights and Reporting leadership team

Collaborate with business stakeholders across Hollard to prioritise, scope out and deliver high-value reporting and analytical initiatives that measurably improve operational performance

Lead complex insights and advanced analytical programs that support business strategy, ameasurable business outcomes and operational efficiencies

Implement continuous improvement processes that reduce reporting cycle times, enhance reliability and user experience 

Establish performance benchmarks and implement feedback loops to ensure all reporting deliverables and benchmarks meet evolving business needs and regulatory requirements

Lead a team by setting clear performance expectations, conducting regular one-on-ones, and creating individual development plans that align with business objectives and career progression

Your knowledge and experience 

Demonstrated experience in an analytical/reporting role within the general insurance industry or financial services with demonstrated ability to deliver measurable business outcomes.

Proven ability to lead and inspire analytical teams through complex projects, fostering a culture of coaching, motivation and high performance.

Strong experience in business intelligence, project management, and reporting with ability to design scalable processes and frameworks that improve team efficiency and stakeholder satisfaction

Advanced technical proficiency in analytical tools (SAS, SQL, Python) with strong statistical capability, experience in visualisation platforms (PowerBI, Tableau) is required.

Strategic thinking capabilities with ability to balance long-term analytical vision with immediate business needs and priorities, ensuring impactful delivery across the organisation.

Working at Hollard

At Hollard, you’ll discover an environment where you can truly make an impact. We offer the best of both worlds – the opportunity, agility and creativity of a company growing with scale, alongside a focus on becoming a catalyst for enduring, positive, social change. 

We’re a tight-knit team, where authentic voices and unique perspectives matter. We work supportively, collaboratively and inclusively to help shape our future and create shared value for our communities. 

Hollard proudly promotes flexible work including remote work, non-traditional work patterns, and work-family balance. We’re also unapologetically committed to creating a workplace that values your diversity with a true sense of inclusion.  We actively seek to hire for your different lived experiences, including as it relates to disability, ethnicity, faith, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, gender diversity, sexual orientation, age, neurodiversity. 

We’re building something special. Working closely with our industry partners to reshape insurance for the better, we’re people changing insurance. 

Please note:  Even if you don’t meet all our selection criteria, we encourage you to apply.  

A full background check will be completed on the successful applicant. 

Visit the Hollard careers page to learn about how Hollard will collect, store and handle your personal information. 

We are NOT engaging Recruitment Agencies for this role and ask agencies to respect our policy of no unsolicited contact regarding advertised vacancies.

Do you want a job that looks different every day? Have you got an unquenchable passion for data and problem solving? Are you ready to jump into a work environment where you'll collaborate with some of the sharpest minds in the industry? We've got a career you'll enjoy.

At InterWorks, we like our work with a hefty side of play. Our team of high achievers is looking for an eager learner who doesn't shy away from hard work. We need someone whose overall data and programming savvy outweighs niche experience with a particular database platform. Whether you've worked primarily in SQL Server, Postgres, or a more modern cloud-based warehouse, all we really care about is that you have a strong understanding of building data pipelines and are ready to keep learning, every single day.

Please be advised that this role is required to be located in the InterWorks Melbourne office in Melbourne, VIC. Remote work or telecommuting arrangements outside of this jurisdiction are not permissible for this position.

Valid work visa or Australian work rights are required for this position.

Salary range commensurate with experience and qualifications: $100,000-$150,000 AUD

What You'll Do

Tackle diverse projects ranging in duration from a few days to a few months, for clients ranging from local Perth businesses to global enterprises and ASX-listed companies

Work with disparate data sources (relational databases, flat files, Excel, HDFS/Big Data systems, high-performance analytical databases, etc.) to unify client data

Collaborate closely with users to understand their unique needs and support them with the best solutions

Solve data-acquisition, integration, and management problems

Create ETL processes based on client needs while managing client expectations

What You'll Need

Must-Haves

Excellent SQL fluency

Strong ETL proficiency using GUI-based tools or code-based patterns

Understanding of data-modelling principles

Passion for delivering compelling solutions that exceed client expectations

Excellent verbal and written communication skills

Strong problem-solving skills

Ability to translate business problems into practical data solutions and work effectively with non-technical stakeholders

A genuine thirst to learn

Adaptability and flexibility in changing situations

What We'd Like You to Have

Experience with modern data-engineering practices and frameworks

Experience with integration from semi-structured file and API sources

Familiarity with tools such as Informatica (IDMC), Matillion, Fivetran, or dbt

Experience with cloud platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, Amazon Redshift, Google BigQuery, or Azure Synapse

What We Offer:

Health Benefits: Our people are our most important asset. That's why we offer an exceptional health insurance subsidy through BUPA, ensuring you and your family are well taken care of.

Wellness Benefits: We want you to stay healthy! Our employees receive an annual allowance to spend on their favourite gym memberships, sports activities, or workout apps.

Work-Life Balance: We offer a life balance allowance, encouraging our employees to relax and recharge — whether with family or going solo.

Tech Choice: Windows or Mac? 13-inch or 15-inch? Lightweight or heavy-duty? You know what works best for you. Our employees have a wide choice of equipment, plus a contribution towards standing desk equipment.

Professional Growth: We invest in our people. You'll have access to ongoing training, certifications, and professional development opportunities to keep your skills sharp and your career moving forward.

Community Impact: We InterWorkers like to use our powers for good. As well as undertaking pro bono consulting work, we support our colleagues' individual charitable passions with dedicated time off for volunteering.

Superannuation: Compulsory superannuation contributions are paid on top of your base salary in accordance with Australian legislation.

Why InterWorks

InterWorks is a people-focused tech consultancy that empowers clients with customized, collaborative solutions, and we love pursuing innovation alongside people who inspire us. Our approach to work and community is unique and unconventional—just like us—and that's the way we want it. The only thing missing is you. At InterWorks, we value unique contributions, our people are the glue that holds our business together. We're always looking for the right people, and we could be your perfect fit.

Technical Business Analyst

Sydney | Hybrid | Immediate Start

A leading Australian digital media organisation is looking for an experienced Technical Business Analyst for an initial 6-month contract. This is an exciting opportunity to work within a high-performing product and technology environment supporting commercial platforms used at scale.

Reporting to the Head of Product - Commercial Platforms, this role sits within the technical delivery team while working closely with Sales and commercial stakeholders. You will play a key role translating business needs into technical solutions, improving processes, and ensuring systems effectively support a fast-moving commercial function.
This role suits a proactive Business Analyst who thrives in dynamic environments, enjoys stakeholder engagement, and is comfortable balancing multiple priorities.

Key Responsibilities
Partner with Product, Technology, and Sales teams to gather, analyse, and document business and technical requirements
Translate commercial and operational needs into clear functional and technical specifications
Facilitate workshops and stakeholder discussions across technical and non-technical teams
Perform SQL troubleshooting and data analysis to support issue investigation and solution validation
Develop detailed process maps and identify opportunities for optimisation and efficiency
Support delivery teams throughout the project lifecycle including discovery, delivery, testing, and rollout
Assist with backlog refinement, prioritisation, and solution clarification
Drive clear communication between business stakeholders and engineering teams
Skills & Experience Required
Proven experience as a Technical Business Analyst within digital, media, technology, or commercial platform environments
Strong SQL skills with experience troubleshooting data or system issues
Advanced process mapping and workflow documentation capabilities
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, particularly with Sales or commercial teams

Experience working alongside Product Managers and Engineering teams in Agile delivery environments
Confident facilitating workshops and managing stakeholder expectations
Highly Regarded
Experience supporting advertising, sales, or commercial platforms
Exposure to media, publishing, or digital content environments
Familiarity with Agile methodologies (Scrum/Kanban) and tools such as Jira or Confluence
Data-driven mindset with strong analytical problem-solving skills
What’s on Offer
6-month contract with potential extension
Hybrid working model based in Sydney
Opportunity to work on high-visibility platforms used by large audiences
Collaborative, fast-moving product and technology environment
Strong leadership exposure working closely with senior product stakeholders

Shape how we build, connect and deliver
At Icon, we’re building more than projects, we’re building a smarter, more connected construction business.

With a strong pipeline across health, education, defence and large scale residential, we’re investing in our digital capability to transform how we plan, deliver and operate projects. This includes modernising our applications, establishing a scalable data platform, and embedding AI and automation into everyday workflows.

We’re looking for an Applications & Digital Manager to assist in leading this journey.
  
The Opportunity
This is a high impact role sitting at the intersection of strategy and delivery.
You will own the applications and digital roadmap, while also stepping in to deliver early outcomes and working solutions. This role is suited to someone who can define the plan — and then roll up their sleeves to make it real.
You’ll work closely with senior leaders, project teams, and functional stakeholders to modernise systems, improve decision making, and embed smarter, more efficient ways of working across the business.
  
What You’ll Be Responsible For:
Applications & Platform Planning

Lead and deliver the applications and digital roadmap aligned to business priorities
Rationalise and optimise our application landscape across corporate, project, and site environments
Establish scalable architecture, integration, and platform standards
Data Platform & Reporting

Lead the establishment of an enterprise data platform (e.g. Snowflake or equivalent)
Integrate data across project, operational, commercial, and finance systems
Enable consistent, self service reporting and dashboards (Power BI)
Improve visibility of project performance, risk, and commercial outcomes
AI & Automation

Identify and deliver practical AI and automation use cases that improve productivity and project outcomes
Drive adoption of Microsoft Copilot and other AI tools across the business
Embed automation into high volume, time intensive workflows with experience in automation tools (e.g. Power Automate, Workato or others).
Delivery & Capability Build

Deliver initial solutions hands on where needed to establish direction and momentum
Build and lead a small, high performing digital team over time
Partner with vendors and internal teams to scale delivery capability
  Governance & Outcomes

Drive standardisation, reduce duplication, and improve system efficiency
Ensure solutions are secure, scalable, and aligned to operational realities
Provide clear reporting on progress, risks, and business outcomes
About You
You’re a pragmatic digital leader who combines structured thinking with hands on delivery.

Proven experience leading applications, data, or digital transformation initiatives
Strong ability to translate business needs into clear, actionable roadmaps
Hands on advanced experience delivering dashboards, integrations, automation, data platform solutions including experience in Snowflake, Data bricks and Power BI.
Experience working across modern Microsoft ecosystems, data platforms, and integration tools, including ETL and API integration (TSQL, Python and JSON).
Comfortable operating in a lean, delivery focused environment
Strong stakeholder engagement skills across technical and operational teams
  Benefits and perks:

Access to our EAP (Employee Assistance Program)
Market competitive Salary and excellent working environment
Excellent Leave Polices that include purchased Annual Leave, Work Life Balance Days, Community Days and generous Parental Leave scheme
Collaborative and values-based workplace culture
Sporting and Social events
Professional Memberships
Discounts on Travel, Retail and Health
How to apply
Applications should be made by submitting a CV and cover letter online outlining your interest, motivation and what you would bring to this role by way of background.

Recruiters, please note we are not engaging agency support on this role unless directly contacted by our internal recruitment team. If you have not been approached, we are not seeking external support at this time. Unsolicited CVs will not be accepted and will be disregarded. Candidates may be contacted directly by our internal team.

What You’ll Be Responsible For:
Applications & Platform Planning

Lead and deliver the applications and digital roadmap aligned to business priorities
Rationalise and optimise our application landscape across corporate, project, and site environments
Establish scalable architecture, integration, and platform standards
Data Platform & Reporting

Lead the establishment of an enterprise data platform (e.g. Snowflake or equivalent)
Integrate data across project, operational, commercial, and finance systems
Enable consistent, self service reporting and dashboards (Power BI)
Improve visibility of project performance, risk, and commercial outcomes
AI & Automation

Identify and deliver practical AI and automation use cases that improve productivity and project outcomes
Drive adoption of Microsoft Copilot and other AI tools across the business
Embed automation into high volume, time intensive workflows with experience in automation tools (e.g. Power Automate, Workato or others).
Delivery & Capability Build

Deliver initial solutions hands on where needed to establish direction and momentum
Build and lead a small, high performing digital team over time
Partner with vendors and internal teams to scale delivery capability
  Governance & Outcomes

Drive standardisation, reduce duplication, and improve system efficiency
Ensure solutions are secure, scalable, and aligned to operational realities
Provide clear reporting on progress, risks, and business outcomes
About You
You’re a pragmatic digital leader who combines structured thinking with hands on delivery.

Proven experience leading applications, data, or digital transformation initiatives
Strong ability to translate business needs into clear, actionable roadmaps
Hands on advanced experience delivering dashboards, integrations, automation, data platform solutions including experience in Snowflake, Data bricks and Power BI.
Experience working across modern Microsoft ecosystems, data platforms, and integration tools, including ETL and API integration (TSQL, Python and JSON).
Comfortable operating in a lean, delivery focused environment
Strong stakeholder engagement skills across technical and operational teams
  Benefits and perks:

Access to our EAP (Employee Assistance Program)
Market competitive Salary and excellent working environment
Excellent Leave Polices that include purchased Annual Leave, Work Life Balance Days, Community Days and generous Parental Leave scheme
Collaborative and values-based workplace culture
Sporting and Social events
Professional Memberships
Discounts on Travel, Retail and Health
How to apply
Applications should be made by submitting a CV and cover letter online outlining your interest, motivation and what you would bring to this role by way of background.

Recruiters, please note we are not engaging agency support on this role unless directly contacted by our internal recruitment team. If you have not been approached, we are not seeking external support at this time. Unsolicited CVs will not be accepted and will be disregarded. Candidates may be contacted directly by our internal team.

Elastic, the Search AI Company, enables everyone to find the answers they need in real time, using all their data, at scale — unleashing the potential of businesses and people. The Elastic Search AI Platform, used by more than 50% of the Fortune 500, brings together the precision of search and the intelligence of AI to enable everyone to accelerate the results that matter. By taking advantage of all structured and unstructured data — securing and protecting private information more effectively — Elastic’s complete, cloud-based solutions for search, security, and observability help organizations deliver on the promise of AI.

What is The Role:


Elastic, the Search Analytics company, is looking for a Principal Solution Architect (pre-sales). Are you looking to make a real impact and play a meaningful role in the growth of our company? As a Solutions Architect (SA) at Elastic, you will serve as a technical authority and trusted advisor to our sales team, customers, partners and community. You will understand and solve our customers’ business issues with the Elastic Stack, and engage the regional Elastic community through events and programs. A successful SA at Elastic will be focused on excellence, taking the initiative to improve both themselves and the team through continuous learning and questioning the status quo.

What You Will Be Doing:

Serving as the technical point of contact for some of our clients
Developing a deep understanding of customers’ goals and objectives, and articulating how our offerings address their needs.
Creating and owning value-based relationships at all levels in customer organisations.
Actively participating in all phases of planning and execution for your territory, from initial discovery to the technical win.
Developing and maintaining a deep understanding of the Elastic products and solutions to demonstrate the value of our offerings in sales meetings and at events such as meetups and conferences.
Advising the sales team on effective ways of positioning Elastic products, solutions and services.
Onboarding, educating and enabling our partners, and supporting them in sales cycles.
Creating collateral, contributing to programs and collaborating with other Elasticians to meet individual client needs.
Being the voice of the customer and community to communicate needs, gaps, and enhancements to our engineering and leadership teams.
Deepening both your sales and technical skills through self-driven education while taking advantage of all the professional development opportunities provided by Elastic.
What You Bring:

A track record of success in a technical presales role – enough experience in selling, demonstrating and implementing technology to earn your customer’s trust.
Experience in at least one of those technologies/solutions is a must: AI enterprise search, observability or cybersecurity
Experience in working with large or mid-size organisations
A demonstrable ability to articulate and sell the benefits of modern platforms, software and technologies.
A real passion for being curious and a continuous learner. You are someone who invests in yourself as much as you invest in your professional relationships.
A history of successful customer relations where you developed an understanding of what made a difference, and devised architectures that helped meet a goal, tackle a problem, or outpace competitors.
An ability to influence. Have you more than once convinced a team you worked for of an idea, technology, or architectural pattern?
The ability to inspire groups, both large and small.
A willingness to travel 30% within a region, as well as occasionally internationally.
Fluent in English (business or native level)
Additional Information - We Take Care of Our People:


As a distributed company, diversity drives our identity. Whether you’re looking to launch a new career or grow an existing one, Elastic is the type of company where you can balance great work with great life. Your age is only a number. It doesn’t matter if you’re just out of college or your children are; we need you for what you can do.

We strive to have parity of benefits across regions, and while regulations differ from place to place, we believe taking care of our people is the right thing to do.

Competitive pay based on the work you do here and not your previous salary
Health coverage for you and your family in many locations
Ability to craft your calendar with flexible locations and schedules for many roles
Generous number of vacation days each year
Increase your impact - We match up to $2000 (or local currency equivalent) for financial donations and service
Up to 40 hours each year to use toward volunteer projects you love
Embracing parenthood with a minimum of 16 weeks of parental leave

Job Ref# 1429225

Category:
Business/Systems Analysts (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
The role: 

You will be responsible for engaging and communicating effectively with stakeholders at every level (Director, Data Manager, Team Lead, Clinicians, and end users) when required to make sure a complete implicit understanding of the business is known. 

You will need to proactively encourage key difficult discussions with the right stakeholders in order to fully comprehend the important aspects of the client's needs and making sure all facets are examined from all angles. This will be important for interpreting and translating technical possibilities into clinical suitability. 

Reporting to the Project Director, you will be responsible for capturing and analysing “As-Is” and “To-Be” business processes models and capturing the business requirements to ensure traceability of requirements throughout the project lifecycle. You will ensure requirements are captured and addressed in the transition of the service from Project to Business As usual whilst providing Business Analysis functions at the Program level, as and when required.

Additionally, you will contribute to an effective client focused team and work collaboratively to initiate, influence and maintain relationships with and between internal and external stakeholders.

Are you right for this role?

To be successful in this role, you must have demonstrated experience performing business analysis activities within IM/ICT projects preferably in the health care industry. You will also have experience with managing stakeholder relationships throughout the project delivery lifecycle, in addition to experience in capturing and analysing “As-Is' and ‘To-Be' business process models across large organisations, eliciting and documenting business and software requirement specifications.

You will have the proven ability to work within a team environment, developing effective working relationships with team members and stakeholders including mentoring other business analysts within the organisation. Proven effective consultation, facilitation, written and oral communication skills for use in a project environment is essential. 

You will also be a self-motivated problem solver, who has proven experience in driving pragmatic decision making and delivering a wide range of documentation to meet the needs of the target audience.

A high level of knowledge of and the ability to provide advice on the application of appropriate business analysis techniques, methodologies and tools, preferably aligned with Business Analysis Body of Knowledge Version 3.0 (BABOK).

About Better Hospital Flow (BHF) Project:

The Queensland Government is investing in improved hospital services through real-time data and targeted technology upgrades under the Health Plan Commitment. A key priority action is Better Hospital Flows (GEC124), which focuses on modernising digital systems to improve hospital workflows, patient flow and visibility of bed availability across the state. Delivery of GEC124 is being progressed through two related initiatives:

Project 3a, which focuses on enhancements to the Statewide Management Information System (SWMIS) to improve the timeliness, quality and consistency of core operational data; and
Project 3b, which focuses on the assessment, planning and implementation of a statewide Patient Flow Insights capability that translates operational data into actionable insights.

Government Election Commitment (GEC) 124 – Better Hospital Flows – Project 3b comprises two phases: Phase 1 - Assessment and Phase 2 - Implementation.
This role relates to Project 3b: Assessment and Implementation and hereafter refers to it as the “project”.

Benefits of working with eHealth Queensland:

By joining eHealth Queensland, you will work within a fast-paced, challenging and supportive environment where every employee plays a critical role in our ongoing success. 

You will enjoy a variety of benefits, including:

Competitive salary + generous superannuation and leave loading
Flexible working arrangements
Diverse work culture
Career training and development

To apply for this exciting opportunity, submit your resume and a one-page cover letter indicating how your experience, abilities, knowledge and personal qualities are relevant for the role, taking into account the key responsibilities and attributes noted in the ‘How you will be assessed?' section.
